title: Procurement and Contract Management Schema
description: Schema-driven procurement and contract lifecycle management for public sector authorities. Anchored to the Procurement Act 2023 (c.54), the National Procurement Policy Statement (2024), and the Local Government Act 1972 s.135. Thresholds reflect the Procurement Act 2023 (Threshold Amounts) (Amendment) Regulations 2025, in force 1 January 2026. v0.2 adds framework management. v0.3 populates GCC constitutional rules from Contract Rules (effective 24 February 2025). v0.3.1 corrects execution authority thresholds. v0.4 adds scheme of delegation and proper officer designations. v0.4.2 removes SIRO and Monitoring Officer from schema — governance roles out of scope for procurement schema. and electronic signature rule per Article 13 of GCC Constitution (constitutional article takes precedence over Contract Rule 18) covering customer, host and joint_host roles.

legislative_basis.title: Procurement Act 2023
legislative_basis.notes: Primary legislation governing public procurement in England, Wales and Northern Ireland above threshold. Replaced PCR 2015.

legislative_basis.title: Procurement Act 2023 (Threshold Amounts) (Amendment) Regulations 2025
legislative_basis.notes: Updates threshold amounts. First review since Act came into force.

legislative_basis.title: Local Government Act 1972
legislative_basis.notes: Requires councils to make standing orders for contracts for the supply of goods or materials or the execution of works.

legislative_basis.title: National Procurement Policy Statement 2024
legislative_basis.notes: Section 13(9) of Procurement Act 2023 requires contracting authorities to have regard to the NPPS. Sets out government priorities: value for money, social value, SME access, net zero.

legislative_basis.title: Public Services (Social Value) Act 2012
legislative_basis.notes: Requires commissioners of public services to consider how procurement might improve economic, social and environmental wellbeing.

legislative_basis.title: Subsidy Control Act 2022
legislative_basis.notes: Applies where contracts may confer a subsidy on a supplier.

thresholds.description: Financial thresholds as amended by SI 2025/1200, in force 1 January 2026. All values inclusive of VAT. Thresholds reviewed every two years to align with UK GPA obligations.

sub_central_authorities.description: Applies to local authorities including district councils
sub_central_authorities.notes: Gloucester City Council is a sub-central authority. Use these thresholds.
sub_central_authorities.source: SI 2025/1200, Schedule 1 PA2023. Gloucester City Council is a sub-central authority.

below_threshold.description: Regulated below-threshold contracts: Part 6 of Act applies. UK7 contract details notice required for all. Duty to consider SMEs (s.86).

de_minimis.description: Contracts below this value may be treated as non-regulated. Local standing orders apply.
de_minimis.notes: Often £10,000 or £25,000 depending on council policy. Must be confirmed.
de_minimis.value_note: Not confirmed. See schema_incomplete_flags I01. Tier 1 lower bound assumed 0 pending confirmation from GCC Financial Regulations.

pipeline_notice_trigger.description: Authorities forecasting >£100m spend must publish a UK1 Pipeline Notice covering procurements >£2m.

kpi_threshold.description: Contracts above £5m must include KPIs and publish annual UK9 Contract Performance Notices.

payment_reporting_threshold.description: Individual payments above £30,000 must be reported in UK17 Payment Compliance Notices.

notice_types.description: Notice taxonomy under Procurement Act 2023. Published on Find a Tender (find-tender.service.gov.uk). All notices carry an Open Contracting Identifier (OCID) creating a golden thread across the procurement lifecycle.

notices.name: Pipeline Notice
notices.trigger: Mandatory where annual procurement spend forecast exceeds £100m. Voluntary where framework value exceeds £2m.
notices.timing: Before procurement activity commences for the year

notices.name: Preliminary Market Engagement Notice
notices.trigger: Before conducting preliminary market engagement with suppliers
notices.notes: Publishing a UK2 can reduce minimum tender period to 10 days.

notices.name: Planned Procurement Notice
notices.trigger: Optional. Signals intent to publish a UK4 Tender Notice at a future date.
notices.timing: Minimum 40 days before UK4 to benefit from reduced tender period

notices.name: Tender Notice
notices.trigger: Required to launch any competitive procurement above or below threshold
notices.notes: Also used for below-threshold competitions. Must be published on Find a Tender.

notices.name: Transparency Notice
notices.trigger: Where a direct award without competition is intended under specific grounds
notices.timing: Before contract signed. Starts 8-working-day standstill period.

notices.name: Contract Award Notice
notices.trigger: On award of contract following competitive tender
notices.timing: Within 30 days of contract award. Triggers 8-working-day standstill period.
notices.notes: Replaces standstill letters under PCR 2015. All bidders notified simultaneously via publication.

notices.name: Contract Details Notice
notices.trigger: After contract signature, for all contracts above threshold and all regulated below-threshold contracts
notices.notes: Must include signed contract document for contracts above £5m.

notices.name: Contract Change Notice
notices.trigger: Before modifying a public contract. Also used for convertible contracts.
notices.timing: Before modification implemented (standstill applies to qualifying modifications)
notices.notes: Modified contract document must be published within 90 days for qualifying modifications (>£5m).

notices.name: Contract Termination Notice
notices.trigger: On termination of a public contract

notices.name: Procurement Termination Notice
notices.trigger: Where contract is not awarded following a tender or transparency notice
notices.timing: As soon as decision made not to proceed
notices.notes: Used to cancel an erroneous UK5 or UK6 notice.

notices.name: Payment Compliance Notice
notices.timing: Within 30 days of end of each reporting period

notice_types.source: PA2023 (PA2023) — sections as noted per notice type. Published on Find a Tender (find-tender.service.gov.uk).

below_threshold_notices.description: Notices for below-threshold contracts. These are GCC/CDP requirements, not PA2023 regulated notices. No UK-code format applies below threshold.

notices.name: Below-Threshold Tender Notice
notices.trigger: Contracts £30,001 to PA2023 threshold where open market sought
notices.platform: Contracts Portal (CDP) or council procurement portal
notices.source: CONTRACT-RULES Rule 9, Local Government Transparency Code 2015

notices.name: Below-Threshold Contract Award Notice
notices.trigger: Contracts £30,001 to PA2023 threshold on award
notices.source: Local Government Transparency Code 2015, CONTRACT-RULES Rule 16

below_threshold_notices.note: Below-threshold notices use BT- prefix codes to distinguish from PA2023 UK-coded notices. The derived_fields.required_notices logic uses these BT codes for the 30000_to_threshold range.

above_threshold.name: Open Procedure
above_threshold.description: Single-stage. Any supplier may submit a full tender. No shortlisting. Selection and award criteria applied to same submission.
use_cases: Where broad market participation is desired
reduction_conditions: UK2 Preliminary Market Engagement Notice published
UK3 Planned Procurement Notice published at least 40 days prior

above_threshold.name: Competitive Flexible Procedure
above_threshold.description: Multi-stage procedure allowing dialogue, negotiation, shortlisting and supplier assessments. Replaces Restricted, Competitive Dialogue, Negotiated and Innovation Partnership procedures.
use_cases: Where specification cannot be fully defined at outset

above_threshold.name: Direct Award
above_threshold.description: Award without competition. Only permissible on specific statutory grounds.
grounds: Extreme urgency (not foreseeable or attributable to contracting authority)
Additional works/services from same supplier where change of supplier not possible
Framework call-off where direct award permitted by framework terms

below_threshold.description: Part 6 of Procurement Act 2023 applies. Lighter-touch regime but transparency and SME duties still apply.
requirements: Publish UK4 below-threshold tender notice on Find a Tender before advertising elsewhere
Publish UK7 Contract Details Notice after award
Consider SMEs in procurement approach (s.86 duty)

frameworks.description: Arrangements with multiple suppliers establishing terms for future call-off contracts.
types: Open framework (can add suppliers, must reopen at least once in 3 years and again at 5 years)
frameworks.open_framework_reopen_obligations: Must reopen to new suppliers at least once within 3 years, and once more within 5 years

dynamic_markets.description: Replaces Dynamic Purchasing Systems. Open list of qualified suppliers from which contracts can be awarded.
dynamic_markets.notes: Can be used below threshold but conditions for membership not permitted below threshold (except specific cases).

procurement_procedures.source: PA2023 ss.19-20 (open and competitive flexible), ss.41-43 (direct award), ss.34-40 (dynamic markets), ss.45-54 (frameworks). GCC Contract Rules s.5 (CONTRACT-RULES).

contract_record.description: Data model for an individual contract record. Each contract should have a corresponding record in the contract register.

contract_id.notes: Internal unique identifier. Separate from OCID.

ocid.description: Open Contracting Identifier from Find a Tender. Links all notices for this procurement.
ocid.required: where notices published on Find a Tender

description.description: Plain English description of what is being procured

direct_award_ground.description: Required if procurement_route is direct_award or below_threshold_direct. Must cite statutory ground or approved waiver reason.

waiver_approved_by.description: Name and role of approver if contract awarded by waiver of procurement rules

supplier.description: Supplier identity. Companies House number is the primary identifier for UK-registered entities.
supplier.source: CONTRACT-RULES Rules 10.8, 14.6, 20, Definitions (SME). PA2023 Schedule 6. Subsidy Control Act 2022.

name.description: Registered trading name of the supplier

companies_house_number.description: UK Companies House registration number. Primary identifier for debarment and subsidy control checks. 8 characters.
companies_house_number.pattern_note: 6-8 alphanumeric: 8 chars for post-1990 numbers, shorter for some older registrations

vat_number.description: VAT registration number. Required for value verification where VAT applicable.

registered_country.description: Country of registration ISO 3166-1 alpha-2. Relevant for treaty state supplier status under PA2023 s.20.

is_uk_or_treaty_supplier.description: Whether supplier is a UK or treaty state supplier under PA2023. Non-UK/non-treaty suppliers may be disregarded under Contract Rule 14.6.

is_sme.description: Whether supplier meets SME definition: fewer than 250 staff AND turnover <= £44m OR balance sheet <= £38m. Source: CONTRACT-RULES Definitions.

debarment_checked.description: Confirms UK Government debarment list checked before above-threshold contract award. Source: CONTRACT-RULES Rule 10.8.

debarment_checked_date.description: Date debarment list checked. Should be immediately prior to award.

debarment_list_url.description: URL of debarment list at time of check.

exclusion_grounds_assessed.description: Confirms mandatory and discretionary exclusion grounds assessed. Source: CONTRACT-RULES Rule 10, PA2023 Schedule 6.

parent_company_name.description: Parent company name where supplier is a subsidiary. Relevant for Parent Company Guarantee. Source: CONTRACT-RULES Rule 20.

beneficial_owner.description: Beneficial owner information where relevant for subsidy control or transparency. Source: Subsidy Control Act 2022.

value_estimated_gbp.description: Estimated contract value inclusive of VAT and any extensions at time of procurement

value_actual_gbp.description: Actual contract value at signature. May differ from estimated.

maximum_contract_duration_months.description: Total maximum duration including all extension options

authorised_signatory.description: Officer authorised to sign contract under scheme of delegation

status_reason.description: Free text reason, especially relevant for terminated_early or cancelled

forward_plan_reference.description: Reference to Forward Plan entry. Required before procurement commences where contract is a key decision (value above £100,000 or meeting KD1/KD2 criteria).

cabinet_decision_reference.description: Reference to Cabinet minute or decision record. Required before contract award where value exceeds £500,000.

cabinet_member_decision_reference.description: Reference to individual Cabinet Member decision record where contract value is £250,001–£500,000. Required before contract award.

contract_performance.description: Performance monitoring framework. KPIs mandatory for contracts above £5m per s.70 PA 2023.

kpis.must_be_agreed: Before or at point of contract signature
kpis.annual_reporting: UK9 Contract Performance Notice required annually

kpi_fields.description: Required where rating is Approaching Target or below

review_schedule.notes: Pre-expiry review at 12 months before expiry (or 18 months for complex/high-value contracts) to allow sufficient lead time for re-procurement.

payment_compliance.legislative_basis: s.88 PA 2023 implies 30-day payment terms into all regulated contracts
payment_compliance.reporting: UK17 Payment Compliance Notice required within 30 days of each reporting period

contract_performance.source: PA2023 ss.70-71 (KPIs and performance notices), s.88 (payment terms). GCC Contract Rule 27 (CONTRACT-RULES).

exclusion_and_debarment.description: PA 2023 introduced mandatory and discretionary exclusion grounds and a centralised Debarment List.

requirements: Consider whether requirements can be structured to facilitate SME participation
Consider whether use of frameworks or lots would assist SMEs
Consider how procurement can improve economic, social and environmental wellbeing
Consider whether to specify social value requirements
Record social value commitments in contract and monitor delivery

supplier_management.source: PA2023 Schedule 6 (exclusion grounds), s.86 (SME duties). Public Services (Social Value) Act 2012 (SV2012). NPPS 2024 (NPPS2024).

approvals_and_governance.description: Approval thresholds, execution authority, and governance requirements derived from Gloucester City Council Contract Rules (effective 24 February 2025). Source: GCC Constitution, Contract Rules, Sections 4, 5, 6 and 18. All values inclusive of VAT.
approvals_and_governance.source_document: GCC Contract Rules, effective 24 February 2025 (PA2023 regime)

contract_letting_tiers.description: Procurement route requirements by estimated total contract value. Source: Contract Rule 5.1. Applies to below-threshold contracts not procured via a compliant Framework Agreement or Dynamic Market.

tiers.label: Budget Holder Discretion
procurement_requirements: Budget Holder may purchase from source offering best value for money
Demonstrable best value — minimum two written quotes where possible
Via Authority's e-tendering system portal where appropriate
Contract by purchase order with relevant standard terms and conditions attached or drawn to supplier's attention
No waiver required unless best value cannot be demonstrated
tiers.contract_form: Purchase order with standard terms and conditions
tiers.legal_involvement: Standard terms and conditions only — no bespoke contract required

tiers.label: Three Quotes — Formal Contract
procurement_requirements: At least three written quotes sought where possible
Via Authority's e-tendering system portal where appropriate
Below-threshold tender notice published on Central Digital Platform before advertising elsewhere
Pre-qualification stage not permitted — appropriate suitability questions may be asked in Request to Quote
Contract details notice published on Central Digital Platform as soon as reasonably practicable after award
tiers.contract_form: Formal written contract prepared or approved by Director of One Legal
tiers.legal_involvement: Director of One Legal must draft or approve contract terms (Contract Rule 4.2.11)
tiers.advertising: Below-threshold tender notice on Central Digital Platform mandatory before advertising elsewhere

tiers.label: Full ITT — Formal Contract
tiers.max_value_gbp_note: Up to but not reaching Threshold (£213,477 for sub-central authority goods/services, £5,372,772 for works)
procurement_requirements: Full competitive procurement via Authority's e-tendering system using Invitation to Tender
Below-threshold tender notice published on Central Digital Platform before advertising elsewhere — with unrestricted full direct internet access to contract documents
Pre-qualification stage not permitted except for works contracts above the supplies/services threshold
For works contracts: Common Assessment Standard (CAS) must be used for pre-qualification
Contract details notice published on Central Digital Platform as soon as reasonably practicable after award
tiers.contract_form: Formal written contract prepared or approved by Director of One Legal
tiers.legal_involvement: Director of One Legal must draft or approve contract terms (Contract Rule 4.2.11)
tiers.works_prequalification: Common Assessment Standard (CAS) required for works contracts

tiers.label: Above Threshold — Full Regulated Procurement
tiers.min_value_gbp_note: At or above Threshold (£213,477 goods/services, £5,372,772 works for sub-central authority)
procurement_requirements: One of the procedures set out in the Legislation must be used: Open Procedure, Competitive Flexible Procedure, or Direct Award where justified
Procurement Adviser, with Authorised Officer and Director of One Legal, to establish appropriate procedure considering value, risk, complexity and market size
Full competitive procurement via e-tendering system using ITT for open or competitive flexible procedures
All PA2023 notice obligations apply (UK4, UK5/UK6, UK7, UK9, UK10, UK11, UK17 as applicable)
8 working day standstill period mandatory before contract execution following UK6 publication
Contract shall not be completed or Purchase Order issued during standstill period
Authorised Officer must notify Procurement Adviser and Director of One Legal immediately on receipt of any challenge during standstill
tiers.contract_form: Formal written contract prepared or approved by Director of One Legal. Executed as deed (common seal) for contracts above £50,000.
tiers.legal_involvement: Director of One Legal mandatory for all above-threshold contracts

execution_authority.description: Who may sign or execute contracts. Source: Article 13.02 of GCC Constitution (takes precedence over Contract Rule 18 where they differ). Note: Article 13 uses 'Council Solicitor' — this is the same officer as 'Director of One Legal' in the Contract Rules, currently employed by Tewkesbury Borough Council under a s.101 Agreement.
execution_authority.conflict_note: Contract Rule 18 states the lower tier threshold as £30,000 and middle tier as £30,001–£50,000. Article 13.02 of the Constitution states £25,000 and £25,001–£50,000 respectively. The Constitution takes precedence. This discrepancy should be resolved in the next Contract Rules review.

tiers.label: Officer delegation
signatories: Any other officer with delegated authority
tiers.form: Signed contract, or sealed if Council Solicitor requires

tiers.label: Council Solicitor sign-off
tiers.form: Signed contract, or sealed if Council Solicitor requires

tiers.label: Deed with common seal
tiers.common_seal_custody: Kept by Tewkesbury Borough Council (One Legal)
common_seal_attestors: Any other person authorised by Council Solicitor
tiers.deed_waiver_note: Council Solicitor may consider it more expedient to sign rather than seal. This is a professional judgement by Council Solicitor, not a general officer discretion. Source: ART13 Article 13.02.
tiers.form_default: Executed as deed with common seal affixed
tiers.form_alternative: Signed contract where Council Solicitor considers more expedient

electronic_signatures.condition: Prior consent of Section 151 Officer, having first obtained advice from Council Solicitor
electronic_signatures.note: Contract Rule 18.4 also lists Monitoring Officer as an approver — Article 13.06 does not. Article 13.06 takes precedence. Discrepancy should be resolved in next Contract Rules review.

legal_proceedings.description: Council Solicitor is authorised to institute, defend or participate in any legal proceedings where necessary to give effect to Council decisions or protect Council's interests

authentication_of_documents.description: Documents necessary to legal procedure or proceedings signed by Managing Director, Council Solicitor, or person authorised by them, unless enactment or Council authority requires otherwise

waiver_rules.description: Circumstances in which Contract Rules may be waived for below-threshold contracts. Source: Contract Rule 6. Waivers are NOT available for above-threshold contracts — Direct Award, Framework or full competition must be used instead.
waiver_rules.above_threshold_waiver: NOT PERMITTED. Above-threshold contracts must use Direct Award (with justification report), Framework Agreement, Dynamic Market, or full competitive tender. Source: Rule 6.2.

permitted_grounds.description: Supplies, works or services of unique or specialised nature, or identical/similar/compatible with existing provision making only one or two sources appropriate — including upgrades or repairs to proprietary machinery where alternative suppliers are not practicable
Supplies are proprietary articles or sold only at fixed prices
Price controlled by trade organisations or no genuine competition for other reasons
Urgency — in opinion of Authorised Officer in consultation with Section 151 Officer and Director of One Legal

Specialist consultants, solicitors, barristers, agents, artists or professional advisers required where: no satisfactory alternative; or evidence indicates no genuine competition; or in Authorised Officer's opinion it is in the Authority's best interest
Authority is purchasing a property or taking a service back in-house with associated contracts, and in Authorised Officer's opinion it is in the Authority's best interests to take on those contracts
Works or supplies can only be carried out or supplied by a statutory body

approval_required.label: Above £250,000
approval_required.urgent_alternative: Head of Paid Service in consultation with Leader of the Authority, Section 151 Officer and Director of One Legal — where urgent decision required and Committee cannot be called

approval_required.label: Up to £250,000
approvers: Budget Holder (if authority delegated) or Head of Paid Service, a Director

waiver_rules.record_keeping: Record of decision and reasons kept on file. Waiver itself kept by Director of One Legal with copy to Procurement Adviser. Source: Rule 6.4.

direct_award_rules.description: Above-threshold direct award. Only permissible on specific statutory grounds listed in PA2023. Source: Contract Rule 5.4.
direct_award_rules.prerequisite: Direct Award Justification Report must be approved before award. Report kept by Director of One Legal.
direct_award_rules.transparency_notice: UK5 Transparency Notice must be published on Central Digital Platform prior to contract award in all cases.

permitted_grounds.description: Prototypes and development — production of a prototype for the purpose of testing the suitability of goods or services
Single supplier — creation or acquisition of a unique work of art or artistic performance
Single supplier — on grounds of intellectual property or exclusive works where there is no reasonable alternative
Single supplier — due to absence of competition for technical reasons where there are no reasonable alternatives
Additional or repeat goods, services or works where difference or incompatibility arising from a change of supplier would result in disproportionate technical difficulties in operation or maintenance

Urgency — goods, services or works strictly necessary for reasons of extreme and unavoidable urgency and as a result cannot be awarded through competitive process. NOTE: urgency is only deemed unavoidable if (1) not attributable to any act or omission of the Authority and (2) could not have been foreseen by the Authority.

User Choice Services — services of a specified kind in regulations under Section 9 of PA2023 supplied for the benefit of a particular individual in respect of which the Authority must have regard to the person in relation to who should provide the service

key_officers.description: Officers with specific roles in GCC procurement governance. Source: Contract Rules definitions and sections 4, 6, 18.

authorised_officer.definition: Officer responsible for carrying out the procurement who has received corporate training on Contract Rules, internal guidance and the Legislation. Source: Rule 4.1, Definitions.
responsibilities: Appraise need for expenditure and priority
Define procurement objectives
Assess and manage procurement risks
Consider most appropriate procurement method including frameworks and collaborative arrangements
Consult users on procurement method, standards and performance monitoring
Check whether a corporate contract already exists
Ensure compliance with s.3 Local Government Act 1999 (duty to consult) where changing services
Ensure compliance with Equality Act 2010
Consider social value obligations (Public Social Value Act 2012) for above-threshold service contracts
Ensure Budget Holder has sufficient budget for contract life
Instruct Director of One Legal in writing to draft or approve contract terms for contracts above £30,000
Raise a purchase order for every contract
Actively manage contract throughout its term
Consider TUPE implications and obtain legal advice before inviting tenders where staff may transfer
Ensure debarment list checked before above-threshold contract award

awarding_officer.definition: Officer with delegated authority to award the contract. Source: Definitions, Rule 16.2.
responsibilities: Award contracts only to Most Advantageous Tender as defined by Award Criteria
Ensure Budget Holder has sufficient funds before award
Sign Tender Acceptance Form or waiver
awarding_officer.note: Consultants may advise but must never make the award decision — this rests solely with the Awarding Officer. Source: Rule 24.4.

budget_holder.definition: Officer responsible for the budget for the relevant procurement. Source: Definitions.
responsibilities: Confirm sufficient budget to sustain contract for its full life
For contracts up to £30,000: may purchase from best value source without formal waiver
Financial propriety and sign-off
Consulted on waivers
Consulted on late tender acceptance
Decision-maker where contract is over budget and no further budget identified
Approval required for electronic signatures
Maintains contracts register for all contracts over £5,000
Decides whether bonds or PCGs required on works contracts above £1m

director_of_one_legal.definition: Senior legal adviser currently employed by Tewkesbury Borough Council acting under a Section 101 Agreement. Source: Definitions.
responsibilities: Draft or approve all contract terms for contracts above £30,000
Execute all contracts above £30,000 (or authorise named person)
Execute all contracts above £50,000 as deed with common seal
Approve electronic signatures
Keep waiver records (copy to Procurement Adviser)
Keep Direct Award Justification Reports
Maintain all contracts above £30,000 (electronic copy to Procurement Adviser)
Consulted on late tender acceptance
Consulted on waivers
Approve new Framework Agreement or Dynamic Market sign-ups (Rule 22.3)
Advise on above-threshold contract modifications before any changes agreed with supplier
director_of_one_legal.note: Shared legal service via Tewkesbury Borough Council s.101 Agreement. LGR implications: this arrangement will need review as part of transition planning.

procurement_adviser.definition: Officer engaged by or on behalf of the Authority to provide procurement services. Source: Definitions.
responsibilities: Lead above-threshold procurement procedures in conjunction with Authorised Officer
Approve new Framework Agreement or Dynamic Market sign-ups (Rule 22.3)
Maintain list of all tenders and quotes received
Receive copy of all contracts above £30,000
Receive copy of all waivers
Consent to e-tendering exceptions below £30,000
Advise on light touch and concession procurements
Notified immediately on receipt of standstill challenge

key_officers.cross_reference: Full statutory proper officer designations are in approvals_and_governance.proper_officers. Full sub-delegation detail is in approvals_and_governance.scheme_of_delegation.

contract_register_rules.description: Contract register requirements from Contract Rules. Source: Rule 19.

contract_storage.above_30000: Kept by Director of One Legal. Electronic copy sent to Procurement Adviser.
contract_storage.below_30000: Kept securely by Budget Holder in accordance with Authority's retention policy.

financial_checks.requirement: Financial checks must be undertaken for all contracts above £10,000 before contract award

bonds_and_guarantees.mandatory_unless: Section 151 Officer considers inappropriate following consultation with Director of One Legal
bonds_and_guarantees.discretionary: May be required for any contract if considered appropriate by Director or Section 151 Officer following consultation with Director of One Legal

embedded_leases_and_derivatives.description: Authorised Officer must notify Section 151 Officer before award where contract contains any of the following. Source: Rule 21.
triggers: Authority will have use or control of, or will be paying for use of, a specific asset or group of assets (embedded lease indicator)
Contract contains a price cap or floor on amounts payable by the Authority (embedded derivative indicator)
Prices increase by more than 2x RPI
Prices increase by some other index or commodity price

tupe_referral.description: Certain procurement decisions must be referred to Cabinet or full Authority. Source: Rule 4.4.
referral_triggers: Contract is above threshold AND TUPE applies to Authority employees OR will result in transfer of staff back to Authority — refer to Cabinet
Contract is over budget AND no further budget identified by Section 151 Officer — refer to Authority (full council)
tupe_referral.exception: Unless otherwise stated elsewhere in the Authority's constitution

scope_exclusions.description: Arrangements to which Contract Rules do not apply. Source: Rule 2.2.
exclusions: Contracts of employment making an individual a direct employee of the Authority
Acquisition, disposal or transfer of land — except where services or works are required as part of the land transaction (e.g. development agreements)
Contracts relating to placement of deposits or raising of loans under treasury management strategy
Agreements between public bodies not relating to works, services or supplies

conflicts_of_interest.legislative_basis: s.83 PA2023, Contract Rule 7, Bribery Act 2010, Code of Conduct for Employees
conflicts_of_interest.definition: An interest includes a personal, professional or financial interest and may be direct or indirect. A conflict arises where a person acting on behalf of the Authority in relation to procurement has such an interest. Source: Rule 7.2.
requirements: Conflicts assessment prepared prior to publication of tender or transparency notice — kept under review and revised as appropriate (Rule 7.3)
All reasonable steps taken to ensure conflicts do not put a supplier at unfair advantage or disadvantage (Rule 7.4)
Declaration of interest form completed by each officer involved in evaluation — held on file by Authorised Officer (Rule 15.3)
No person with personal or financial interest in any candidate may be involved in influencing the award decision — applies regardless of contract value (Rule 15.3)
Mitigation may include removal of individual from process or removal of supplier from process (Rule 7.5)

evaluation_rules.award_criteria_basis: Most Advantageous Tender (MAT) only. Source: Rule 11.1, Definitions.
mat_assessment_methods: Cost (cost-effectiveness basis e.g. asset life-cycle assessment)
Best Price/Quality Ratio (quality including technical merit, aesthetic and functional characteristics, delivery, after-sales service, environmental and social matters)
award_criteria_requirements: Disclosed in Invitation to Tender or Request for Quote
Must relate to subject-matter of contract
Must be sufficiently clear, measurable and specific
Must not breach rules on technical specifications (e.g. no specific trademarks or place of origin without necessity)
Must be a proportionate means of assessing tenders having regard to nature, complexity and cost
Must be accompanied by assessment methodology describing how tenders will be assessed
Where more than one criterion: relative importance must be expressed by weighting as percentage, ranking in order of importance, or other clear description
Criteria strictly observed throughout the award procedure by all officers involved
evaluation_rules.social_value: Can be included within quality element of award criteria. Source: Rule 11.2.
evaluation_rules.site_visits_presentations: If used as part of evaluation: must be stated in ITT with clear indication of whether scored separately or used to moderate scores. If scored separately, criteria and weighting must be disclosed. Source: Rule 15.4.

lots_policy.description: GCC has a duty to consider lot structure to reduce barriers for SMEs. Source: Rule 12.
lots_policy.duty: Must consider whether goods, services or works could reasonably be supplied under more than one contract and whether contracts could appropriately be awarded by reference to lots
lots_policy.notice_requirement: Tender notice and documents must state whether lots are suitable or provide justification for not using them

collaborative_procurement.description: GCC may participate in collaborative or joint procurement with other local authorities or public bodies. Source: Rule 23.
collaborative_procurement.includes: Membership or use of a Purchasing Consortia
collaborative_procurement.framework_sign_up: Approval of Procurement Adviser or Director of One Legal required before signing up to any new Framework Agreement or Dynamic Market. Source: Rule 22.3.

assessment_summaries.mandatory: For all suppliers that submitted an assessed tender as required by Legislation (above-threshold competitive procurements)
assessment_summaries.below_threshold: Not mandatory for below-threshold procurements or framework call-offs (unless framework terms require it). Best practice to provide for all procurements in line with procurement objective of sharing information.

contract_modifications.above_threshold: Can only be changed if permitted by Legislation. Legal advice from Director of One Legal must be sought before agreeing any changes with supplier. Source: Rule 26.1.
contract_modifications.below_threshold: In limited circumstances and with regard to Legislation and advice from Director of One Legal, Awarding Officer may authorise changes including extensions not already provided for. Source: Rule 26.2.

scheme_of_delegation.description: Sub-delegations to officers from the Managing Director. Source: Scheme of Sub-Delegation (signed by Managing Director). Part 3 of Constitution sets out powers delegated to Managing Director and Directors. This scheme sets out sub-delegations from the Managing Director to Heads of Service.
scheme_of_delegation.source_document: Scheme of Sub-Delegation, GCC Constitution Part 3

award_authority_by_officer.description: All Heads of Service listed below share a common tender/quotation acceptance limit. This is the key awarding officer threshold for procurement purposes.

additional_procurement_powers: Approve waiver to Contract Rules where value does not exceed £250,000
Monitor performance of contracts and service level agreements for non-internally provided services within remit
service_areas: Council Tax, NNDR and other Revenues
Investigation of fraud and financial impropriety
Review of listings for Assets of Community Value
officers_with_award_authority.notes: Only Head with explicit waiver approval authority up to £250,000 and operational control of Procurement. Also has delegated authority for budget virements up to £25,000, ex gratia payments up to £5,000, debt write-offs under £10,000.

Commissioning and digital transformation remit. Strategic Commissioning and Data Controller functions relevant to contracts involving data processing — but SIRO and information governance responsibilities are out of scope for this schema.
Planning and development remit. Section 106 agreements determined jointly with Director One Legal.

above_250000.description: Contracts above £250,000 require Director or above. Specific thresholds for Director and Cabinet/Council level not set out in this Scheme — refer to Part 3 of Constitution and Cabinet/Council delegation tables.
above_250000.waiver_above_250000: Requires appropriate Cabinet Member (or Head of Paid Service in consultation with Leader, s151 Officer and Director of One Legal in urgent cases). Source: Contract Rule 6.3.1.
above_250000.TODO: Obtain Part 3 Constitution delegation tables to complete the picture above £250,000

waiver_approval_authority.description: Resolves the waiver approval tiers from Contract Rule 6.3. Now confirmed by Scheme of Sub-Delegation.

tiers.label: Up to £250,000
tiers.approver: Head of Finance and Resources (as Budget Holder with delegated authority) or Head of Paid Service, a Director
tiers.source: Contract Rule 6.3.2, Scheme of Sub-Delegation (Head of Finance and Resources)

tiers.label: Above £250,000
tiers.urgent_alternative: Head of Paid Service in consultation with Leader of the Authority, Section 151 Officer and Director of One Legal

waiver_approval_authority.note: Waivers not available above threshold. Above-threshold contracts must use Direct Award, Framework, Dynamic Market or full competitive tender. Source: Contract Rule 6.2.

funding_bid_authority.description: Delegation to Heads for funding bids with resource implications

proper_officers.description: Statutory Proper Officer designations relevant to procurement and contract governance. Source: Proper Officers document, GCC Constitution.

officers.legislative_basis: s.4 Local Government and Housing Act 1989
procurement_relevant_functions: Appointment of Proper Officers (s.270(3) LGA 1972)
Emergency powers — authority to exercise Council powers in cases of emergency (s.138 LGA 1972)
Above £250,000 waiver urgent approval — in consultation with Leader, s151 Officer and Director of One Legal
officers.notes: Managing Director holds Head of Paid Service designation at GCC

officers.legislative_basis: s.151 Local Government Act 1972, Local Government Finance Act 1988
statutory_functions: Responsible for proper administration of the Authority's financial affairs (s.151 LGA 1972)
Must issue report to Members if there is or is likely to be unlawful expenditure or unbalanced budget (s.114 LGFA 1988)
Report to Council annually on robustness of estimates and financial reserves (LGA 2003)
Adequate and effective system of internal audit (Accounts and Audit Regulations 2011)
Certification of relevant powers to enter into contracts (Local Government (Contracts) Act 1997)
procurement_relevant_functions: Financial sign-off and budget confirmation for all significant contracts
Consulted on waivers at all tiers
Consulted on late tender acceptance
Decides whether bonds or PCGs required on works contracts above £1m
Maintains contracts register for all contracts above £5,000
Approval of electronic signatures (having obtained advice from Council Solicitor)
Referred contracts over budget where no further budget identified
Referred above-threshold TUPE contracts
Certification of relevant powers to enter into contracts under Local Government (Contracts) Act 1997
Civil Evidence Act 1995 — certify Council records for admitting documents in civil proceedings

officers.legislative_basis: s.101 Agreement with Tewkesbury Borough Council
procurement_relevant_functions: Execute all contracts above £25,000 (or authorise named person) — Article 13.02
Execute all contracts above £50,000 as deed with common seal — Article 13.02
Draft or approve all contract terms above £30,000 — Contract Rule 4.2.11
Keep Direct Award Justification Reports — Contract Rule 5.4
Keep waiver records (copy to Procurement Adviser) — Contract Rule 6.4
Keep all contracts above £30,000 (electronic copy to Procurement Adviser) — Contract Rule 19.3
Consulted on late tender acceptance — Contract Rule 14.5
Consulted on waivers at all tiers — Contract Rule 6.3
Approve new Framework Agreement or Dynamic Market sign-ups — Contract Rule 22.3
Advise on above-threshold contract modifications before any changes agreed with supplier — Contract Rule 26.1
Custody of Common Seal (held at Tewkesbury Borough Council) — Article 13.05
Institute, defend or participate in legal proceedings — Article 13.03
officers.lgr_risk: This is a shared service via s.101 Agreement with Tewkesbury Borough Council. Tewkesbury is itself subject to LGR. The continuation and governance of this arrangement post-vesting day requires urgent legal and constitutional review.

proper_officers.scope_note: This section covers only officers with direct procurement or contract execution responsibilities. Monitoring Officer and SIRO are excluded — they are governance roles relevant to other schemas (information governance, constitutional governance) not to procurement decisions.

decision_making.description: Decision-making thresholds and types relevant to procurement. Sources: Article 12 GCC Constitution, Part 3E Executive Functions (Table 4 and Appendix A).
source_documents: Part 3E — Executive Functions, Table 4 and Appendix A
principles: Proportionality — action should be proportionate to the desired outcome
Due consideration of professional advice from officers
Clarity of aims and desired outcomes

officer_key_decision_restriction.rule: An Officer does not have delegated authority to take a Key Decision unless (a) specifically authorised to do so by the Leader, or (b) the Managing Director is taking an urgent decision as set out in Part 3E.
officer_key_decision_restriction.procurement_implication: Officers may award contracts in the £100k–£250k space (which are technically key decisions under KD3) only where the Leader has specifically authorised it — in practice via the Forward Plan entry mechanism.

officer_limit.rule: Accept tenders and quotations within budget and not exceeding £250,000

cabinet_member_limit.rule: Accept tenders within budget and exceeding £250,000
cabinet_member_limit.authority: Cabinet Member (specific Table 4 delegation — overrides Appendix A general ceiling per lex specialis)
cabinet_member_limit.upper_bound: Up to £500,000 — above this KD4 triggers full Cabinet requirement

full_cabinet.source: PART3E Table 4 — Make Key Decision: Cabinet, ART12 KD4

waiver_table4.officer_limit: Approve waiver to Contract Rules where value does not exceed £250,000 — MD/D
waiver_table4.cabinet_member_urgent: Approve waiver from Contract Rules where value exceeds £250,000 and urgent decision required — MD only (Contract Rule 6.3.1)
waiver_table4.cabinet_limit: Approve waiver to Contract Rules where value is in excess of £250,000 — Cabinet Member
waiver_table4.source: Part 3E, Table 4 — Commissioning and Contracts, Contract Rule 6.3

general_rule.rule: Individual Cabinet Members cannot make decisions involving expenditure or savings exceeding £250,000.

specific_carve_out.rule: Table 4 (Commissioning and Contracts) explicitly delegates 'Accept tenders within budget and exceeding £250,000' to Cabinet Member. This is a named specific function delegation.

constitutional_interpretation.principle: Lex specialis — specific delegation overrides general rule. The Table 4 tender acceptance delegation is a specific carve-out from the Appendix A general ceiling. Cabinet Members may therefore accept tenders above £250,000 (up to the KD4 full Cabinet threshold of £500,000) by virtue of the Table 4 specific delegation, notwithstanding the general Appendix A ceiling.
constitutional_interpretation.source: PART3E Table 4 read with PART3E Appendix A para 2(3)

full_cabinet_trigger.rule: Above £500,000 the KD4 key decision threshold is triggered and the function passes to full Cabinet regardless of Table 4 Cabinet Member delegation.
full_cabinet_trigger.source: ART12 12.03(b)(iv), PART3E Table 4 — Make Key Decision: Cabinet

key_decision.description: Executive function decisions meeting any of the following criteria. Must follow Executive Procedure Rules and appear on the Forward Plan.
key_decision.forward_plan: Leader publishes Forward Plan at least monthly showing 12-month programme including Key Decisions. Source: Part 3E, Appendix A, para 2.

triggers.description: Expenditure or saving significant having regard to the budget for the service or function
triggers.procurement_note: Qualitative test — applies where a contract represents a significant proportion of a service budget even if below monetary thresholds

triggers.description: Likely to have a significant impact on two or more wards
triggers.procurement_note: Relevant to city-wide contracts — waste, parks, CCTV, customer services

triggers.description: Budget expenditure or budget savings in excess of £100,000
triggers.procurement_note: Makes contracts above £100k technically key decisions requiring Forward Plan entry. However, award authority for contracts up to £250k remains with officers where Leader has authorised via Forward Plan mechanism (3E.11).

triggers.description: Contract valued in excess of £500,000, or acquisition/disposal of land with value in excess of £250,000
triggers.procurement_note: Primary full Cabinet threshold for contract decisions. Above £500,000 requires full Cabinet approval.

urgent_decision.description: Decision meeting urgency criteria in Article 12.03(c).
urgent_decision.urgent_key_decision: Leader or Cabinet Member only — MD can take urgent key decisions only where specifically permitted. Source: Part 3E Table 4.
urgent_decision.urgent_officer_decision: MD may take urgent non-key decisions where insufficient time for Leader/Cabinet Member consideration — reported to next Cabinet meeting. Source: Part 3E Table 5.
urgent_decision.procurement_note: Urgency does not remove procurement compliance obligations. Above-threshold direct awards still require Direct Award Justification Report and UK5 Transparency Notice.

procurement_decision_authority_matrix.description: Definitive procurement decision authority matrix for GCC. Synthesised from Contract Rules (Rule 5.1), Scheme of Sub-Delegation, Part 3E Table 4, Appendix A, and Article 12. All values inclusive of VAT.

matrix.label: Budget Holder discretion
matrix.process: Best value — minimum two quotes where possible. Purchase order with standard T&Cs. No waiver required unless best value cannot be demonstrated.

matrix.label: Three quotes — officer award
matrix.award_authority_label: Authorised Officer / Head of Service (sub-delegated, ceiling £250,000)
matrix.process: Three written quotes. Below-threshold tender notice on CDP. Formal contract via One Legal.

matrix.label: Full ITT — officer award
matrix.award_authority_label: Authorised Officer / Head of Service (sub-delegated, ceiling £250,000)
matrix.process: Full ITT via e-tendering. Below-threshold tender notice on CDP. Formal contract via One Legal. Deed if above £50,000.

matrix.label: Officer decision space — key decision
matrix.award_authority_label: Officer (MD or Director — sub-delegated to Heads of Service, ceiling £250,000)
matrix.forward_plan_note: Officers may award under 3E.11 where Leader has authorised — Forward Plan entry is the authorisation mechanism. Without Forward Plan entry the officer lacks authority to award even within financial limit.
matrix.compliance_note: LIVE COMPLIANCE QUESTION (C3): GCC may not currently be applying Forward Plan requirement to contracts in this range. See source_documents.known_conflicts.C3 and risk flag R11.

above_threshold_logic.description: Whether a contract in this tier is above threshold depends on contract type. Evaluate against thresholds.sub_central_authorities.

rules.above_threshold_if: value_estimated_gbp >= 663540 — no contracts in tier 4 will reach this
value_estimated_gbp >= 5372772 — no contracts in tier 4 will reach this

matrix.label: Cabinet Member decision space
sources: PART3E Table 4 — 'Accept tenders within budget and exceeding £250,000: Cabinet Member'
PART3E Appendix A para 2(3) — general ceiling, overridden by specific Table 4 delegation
matrix.cabinet_member_delegation_basis: Specific delegation under PART3E Table 4: 'Accept tenders within budget and exceeding £250,000 — Cabinet Member'. This is a specific carve-out that overrides the general Appendix A para 2(3) ceiling of £250,000 per the principle of lex specialis (specific rule overrides general rule). The Table 4 delegation governs; the Appendix A ceiling applies to other Cabinet Member decisions not subject to a specific Table 4 function delegation.

matrix.label: Full Cabinet — key decision
matrix.reference_field_note: cabinet_decision_reference is required. cabinet_member_decision_reference is NOT sufficient for tier 6 — these are distinct constitutional requirements.
matrix.key_decision_triggers_note: KD3 (value > £100,000) applies to all contracts in this tier in addition to KD4. Both triggers are active. KD4 is the primary trigger that requires full Cabinet; KD3 is also satisfied and is noted for completeness. Source: ART12 12.03(b)(iii) and (iv).

waiver_matrix.approver: MD/Director (sub-delegated to Head of Finance and Resources)
waiver_matrix.source: Part 3E Table 4, Contract Rule 6.3.2, Scheme of Sub-Delegation

notes: KD1 and KD2 are qualitative tests that can make any contract a key decision regardless of value — must be assessed at procurement planning stage
Officer award authority in tier 4 depends on Leader authorisation via Forward Plan mechanism (3E.11) — without Forward Plan entry the officer technically lacks authority to award
Individual Cabinet Members cannot make decisions exceeding £250,000 expenditure — tenders above £250,000 go to full Cabinet not individual Cabinet Member
The £100,000 KD3 threshold and the £250,000 officer award ceiling are distinct — KD3 triggers Forward Plan requirement, the £250,000 ceiling triggers escalation from officer to Cabinet Member

evaluator.description: Logic for determining tier and compliance requirements from contract value. All values inclusive of VAT.
pseudocode: tier = first tier where min_value_gbp <= value_estimated_gbp <= (max_value_gbp or infinity)
if tier.key_decision and not forward_plan_reference: flag R11
if value_estimated_gbp > 250000 and not cabinet_member_decision_reference: flag R12
if value_estimated_gbp > 500000 and not cabinet_decision_reference: flag R13
if value_estimated_gbp > threshold[contract_type] and procurement_route not in regulated_routes: flag R08

contract_register.description: Requirements for GCC's contracts register. Source: Contract Rule 19.2 and Local Government Transparency Code 2015.
contract_register.legislative_basis: Contract Rule 19.2, Local Government Transparency Code 2015, PA2023 transparency obligations
contract_register.publication_requirement: All contracts above £5,000 must be included in the register. Transparency Code requires publication of contracts above £5,000. Above-threshold contracts additionally require Find a Tender notice publication.
contract_register.update_frequency: At least annually, and on any material change
contract_register.platform: TODO - confirm publication platform (council website and/or Contracts Finder). Note: Find a Tender notices satisfy statutory publication obligations for above-threshold contracts.

procurement_lifecycle_stages.name: Needs Definition and Business Case
procurement_lifecycle_stages.description: Define the requirement, confirm budget, assess whether existing contracts or frameworks can be used, consider make-vs-buy.
key_actions: Produce specification / scope of requirements
Estimate contract value (whole life, including extensions and VAT)
Identify correct procurement threshold
Check existing frameworks
Complete social value assessment
Consider SME-friendly structuring
Confirm budget with Section 151 Officer

procurement_lifecycle_stages.name: Market Engagement
procurement_lifecycle_stages.description: Optional but recommended pre-procurement engagement with the market to refine requirements and test market appetite.
key_actions: Publish UK2 Preliminary Market Engagement Notice before engaging
Hold supplier events, issue RFIs, or conduct desk research
Document engagement and how it has shaped requirements
Manage conflicts of interest from engaged suppliers

procurement_lifecycle_stages.name: Procurement
procurement_lifecycle_stages.description: Formal competitive or direct award process
key_actions: Finalise procurement documents (specification, award criteria, contract terms)
Publish UK4 Tender Notice (or UK5 Transparency Notice for direct award)
Manage clarifications
Evaluate tenders against published criteria
Check exclusion grounds and debarment list
Issue Assessment Summaries to all bidders
Publish UK6 Contract Award Notice
Observe 8-working-day standstill period

procurement_lifecycle_stages.name: Contract Award and Mobilisation
procurement_lifecycle_stages.description: Formalise the contract, ensure supplier is ready to deliver.
key_actions: Execute contract (signed by authorised signatory)
Publish UK7 Contract Details Notice
Issue purchase order
Conduct supplier mobilisation meeting
Agree KPIs (mandatory >£5m)
Set up monitoring and review schedule
Establish data processing agreement if supplier handles personal data

procurement_lifecycle_stages.name: Contract Management
procurement_lifecycle_stages.description: Ongoing management of contract performance, payments and compliance.
key_actions: Conduct performance reviews per schedule
Monitor KPIs and publish UK9 annually (>£5m contracts)
Publish UK17 Payment Compliance Notices each reporting period
Manage change control - publish UK10 Contract Change Notice before modifications
Escalate poor performance through contract mechanisms
Maintain contract register entry

procurement_lifecycle_stages.name: Expiry or Termination
procurement_lifecycle_stages.description: Planned expiry or early termination of contract.
key_actions: Trigger pre-expiry review at 12 months (18 months for complex contracts)
Decide whether to extend, re-procure, or exit
Manage transition / handover to new supplier
Publish UK11 Contract Termination Notice within 30 days
Conduct lessons learned review
Update contract register

risk_flags.description: Schema-encoded risk indicators for procurement and contract records. Can be used to drive dashboards and exception reports.

flags.label: Expiring within 6 months
flags.logic: expiry_date within 180 days of today and status in [live, extension_active]

flags.label: Expiring within 3 months
flags.logic: expiry_date within 90 days of today and status in [live, extension_active]

flags.label: No active contract manager
flags.logic: responsible_officer is null or blank

flags.label: KPI reporting overdue
flags.logic: value_actual_gbp > 5000000 and last_uk9_published > 13 months ago

flags.label: Contract performance Requires Improvement or Inadequate
flags.logic: any current KPI rating in [Requires Improvement, Inadequate] and no remediation_plan recorded

flags.label: Direct award without documented ground
flags.logic: procurement_route in [direct_award, below_threshold_direct] and direct_award_ground is null

flags.label: High value below-threshold (potential split contract risk)
flags.logic: value_estimated_gbp > 150000 and procurement_route = below_threshold_direct
flags.notes: Scrutiny for potential contract splitting contrary to s.86 PA 2023

flags.label: Payment compliance notice overdue
flags.logic: last_uk17_published > 31 days after end of last reporting period

flags.label: Contract modified without UK10 notice
flags.logic: contract_value_changed = true and no linked UK10 notice recorded

flags.label: Extension exercised without procurement assessment
flags.logic: extension_exercised = true and no pre_expiry_review recorded

flags.label: Key decision (KD3) — no Forward Plan reference recorded
flags.logic: value_estimated_gbp > 100000 AND forward_plan_reference is null AND status in [pipeline, in_procurement]
flags.note: Contracts above £100k are key decisions under KD3. Forward Plan entry required before procurement commences.

flags.label: Tier 5 — Cabinet Member decision reference missing
flags.logic: value_estimated_gbp > 250000 AND value_estimated_gbp <= 500000 AND cabinet_member_decision_reference is null AND status not in [pipeline]
flags.note: Applies to tier 5 contracts (£250,001–£500,000) only. For tier 6 contracts (above £500,000) use R13 — cabinet_decision_reference is the appropriate reference. R12 and R13 are mutually exclusive by tier. A cabinet_decision_reference does NOT satisfy the R12 requirement — tier 6 contracts require both a Cabinet decision (R13) and, where the procurement straddled tier 5, a Cabinet Member decision record for that phase.

flags.label: Full Cabinet key decision — no Cabinet minute reference recorded
flags.logic: value_estimated_gbp > 500000 AND cabinet_decision_reference is null AND status not in [pipeline]
flags.note: Applies to tier 6 contracts (above £500,000). Cabinet minute reference required before award. cabinet_decision_reference is distinct from cabinet_member_decision_reference — the latter is not sufficient for tier 6.

frameworks.description: Framework agreements relevant to GCC procurement. Covers three distinct roles: customer (GCC calls off from a framework established by another authority or body), host (GCC establishes and manages a framework, potentially for use by other contracting authorities), and joint_host (GCC co-establishes a framework with one or more other authorities). Each role carries different legal obligations under the Procurement Act 2023.

customer.description: GCC is a named contracting authority permitted to call off from a framework established by a central purchasing body or another contracting authority.
gcc_obligations: Verify GCC is a named or eligible contracting authority before calling off
Call off in accordance with framework-specific rules (direct award vs mini-competition)
Publish UK6 and UK7 notices for each call-off contract
Verify framework has not expired before calling off
Verify supplier remains on framework lot at point of call-off
Check supplier against debarment list at call-off stage
compliance_risks: Calling off from an expired framework
Calling off from a lot GCC is not named on
Direct awarding when framework terms require mini-competition
Calling off a supplier who has been removed from the framework

host.description: GCC establishes and manages a framework agreement. GCC acts as the contracting authority and central purchasing body. Other contracting authorities may be named as entitled to call off.
gcc_obligations: Run compliant competitive procurement to establish framework (open or competitive flexible procedure)
Publish full notice lifecycle: UK4, UK6, UK7 at establishment
Define call-off rules clearly in framework terms
Maintain supplier list and manage additions/removals
For open frameworks: reopen to new suppliers at least once within 3 years and once within 5 years (s.34-40 PA 2023)
Publish UK10 Contract Change Notice for qualifying framework modifications
Notify entitled contracting authorities of any material changes
Manage framework expiry and succession planning
Publish UK11 Contract Termination Notice on framework closure
Consider cost recovery model if other authorities call off

entitled_authority_management.description: If other contracting authorities are entitled to call off, GCC must maintain a list of entitled authorities and communicate framework terms and changes to them.

cost_recovery.description: GCC may charge entitled authorities a management fee or percentage levy on call-off spend. Must be transparent and agreed in framework terms.
cost_recovery.notes: Cost recovery model must be set at framework establishment. Cannot be introduced retrospectively.

lgr_implications.description: Frameworks hosted by GCC as a district council may need to be novated to the new unitary authority on vesting day. Entitled authorities using GCC-hosted frameworks must be notified. Legal advice required.
actions: Identify all GCC-hosted frameworks before vesting day
Seek legal advice on novation or re-establishment requirement
Notify entitled authorities of LGR transition
Assess whether framework terms permit novation or require fresh procurement

joint_host.description: GCC co-establishes a framework with one or more other contracting authorities. One authority typically acts as lead contracting authority for notice publication purposes. All joint hosts share governance obligations.
gcc_obligations: All obligations under host role apply
Agree governance arrangements with co-hosts in a collaboration agreement
Identify lead contracting authority for notice publication
Agree cost sharing and liability arrangements
Maintain alignment with co-hosts on framework changes and supplier management

framework_record.description: Data model for a framework agreement record. Applicable to all three roles. Role-specific fields are conditional.

name.description: Full name of the framework agreement

role.description: GCC's role in relation to this framework

category.description: Nature of what the framework covers

subject_matter.description: Plain English description of what the framework covers

provider.description: The authority that established the framework. For host/joint_host role this is GCC.

established_under.description: Procurement rules under which framework was established. PCR2015 frameworks remain valid for their duration.

framework_type.description: Closed frameworks have a fixed supplier list. Open frameworks must admit new suppliers periodically under PA2023.

open_framework_reopen_obligations.description: Required for open frameworks established under PA2023

maximum_duration_months.description: Maximum 48 months for most frameworks, 96 months for open frameworks under PA2023

successor_framework_id.description: Framework ID of successor framework if this one has been superseded

suppliers.description: Suppliers on this lot. Required for host/joint_host role. Optional but useful for customer role.

call_off_rules.description: Rules governing how call-offs must be made from this lot

direct_award_threshold_gbp.description: Maximum value for direct award. Null means no cap.

mini_competition_required_above_gbp.description: Mini-competition mandatory above this value. Null means always permitted to direct award.

minimum_quotes_for_direct_award.description: Some frameworks require minimum number of supplier quotes even for direct award

gcc_access.description: GCC-specific access details. Required for customer role.

contracts_called_off.description: Contract IDs of GCC call-off contracts under this framework

entitled_authorities.description: Other contracting authorities entitled to call off. Required for host/joint_host role.

cost_recovery.description: Cost recovery model for hosted frameworks

rate.description: Fee amount or percentage as applicable

gcc_lead.description: True if GCC is the lead contracting authority

notices.description: Find a Tender notices associated with this framework. Primarily relevant for host/joint_host role.

responsible_officer.description: Officer responsible for managing GCC's relationship with this framework

lgr_risk.description: LGR transition risk assessment for this framework

risk_level.description: Low: customer frameworks with no transition impact. Medium: active call-offs in flight at vesting day. High: hosted frameworks with entitled authorities.

framework_risk_flags.description: Risk flags specific to framework management, extending the contract-level risk flags.

flags.label: Framework expired — active call-offs may be non-compliant
flags.logic: framework.status = expired AND any linked contract.status in [live, extension_active]

flags.label: Framework expiring within 6 months
flags.logic: framework.expiry_date within 180 days and framework.status = active

flags.label: Framework expiring within 3 months
flags.logic: framework.expiry_date within 90 days and framework.status = active

flags.label: Non-compliant call-off — direct award above threshold
flags.logic: contract.procurement_route = framework_call_off_direct AND contract.value_actual_gbp > lot.call_off_rules.mini_competition_required_above_gbp

flags.label: Call-off from lot GCC not approved to use
flags.logic: contract.framework_lot not in framework.gcc_access.lots_accessible

flags.label: Hosted framework — open reopen obligation overdue
flags.logic: framework.role in [host, joint_host] AND framework.framework_type = open AND open_framework_reopen_obligations.first_reopen_due < today AND first_reopen_completed = false

flags.label: Hosted framework — no successor identified with <6 months to expiry
flags.logic: framework.role in [host, joint_host] AND framework.expiry_date within 180 days AND successor_framework_id is null

flags.label: Hosted framework — LGR high risk, no action recorded
flags.logic: framework.role in [host, joint_host] AND lgr_risk.risk_level = high AND lgr_risk.action_required is null

flags.label: Call-off from PCR2015 framework — verify framework still valid
flags.logic: framework.established_under = PCR2015 AND framework.status = active
flags.notes: PCR2015 frameworks remain valid for their duration but cannot be extended or re-opened under PA2023. Worth confirming expiry date.

flags.label: Supplier called off is no longer on framework lot
flags.logic: contract.supplier matches a supplier record on the lot where supplier.status != active at contract.start_date

known_framework_providers.description: Reference list of commonly used framework providers for local authorities. Not exhaustive. Used to validate framework_record.provider entries.

providers.name: Crown Commercial Service
providers.notes: Central government CPB. Wide range of frameworks covering IT, professional services, fleet, facilities, HR. Most accessible to local authorities.

providers.name: Eastern Shires Purchasing Organisation
providers.notes: Local authority owned CPB. Strong on facilities, cleaning, catering, stationery, energy.

providers.name: Yorkshire Purchasing Organisation
providers.notes: Local authority owned CPB. Education, facilities, HR, IT.

providers.name: North Eastern Universities Purchasing Consortium
providers.notes: Primarily HE sector but some frameworks open to public sector.

providers.name: East Midlands Councils
providers.notes: Regional consortium. Legal services, HR, ICT. Used in register sample (GCC-2021-0003).

providers.name: Local Partnerships
providers.notes: LGA and HM Treasury joint venture. Frameworks for commercial and project management services.

providers.name: Local Government Association
providers.notes: Some frameworks and commercial arrangements available to member authorities.

providers.name: NHS Workforce Alliance
providers.notes: Primarily NHS but non-clinical staffing frameworks open to wider public sector. Used in register sample (GCC-2025-0044).

providers.name: Gloucester City Council (hosted frameworks)
providers.notes: Placeholder for any frameworks established and hosted by GCC. No active hosted frameworks at schema v0.1.

lgr_considerations.description: Specific considerations relevant to Local Government Reorganisation in Gloucestershire. TODO - expand as LGR programme develops.

issues.label: Contract novation on transfer
issues.description: Where functions transfer to a new unitary authority, contracts held by district councils may need to be novated to the new authority. Novation requires supplier consent. Early engagement recommended.
issues.action: Identify all contracts that would transfer, assess novation risk, engage suppliers early

issues.label: Harmonisation of procurement frameworks
issues.description: District councils may use different frameworks and standing orders. The new authority will need a single procurement policy from vesting day.
issues.action: Map current framework usage across districts; identify gaps and conflicts

issues.label: Contract register completeness
issues.description: Each district must have a complete, accurate contract register before LGR transition. Incomplete registers create legal and financial risk for the new authority.
issues.action: Audit contract registers across all districts; standardise on common schema

issues.label: In-flight procurements
issues.description: Procurements that straddle the LGR vesting date create legal uncertainty about which authority is contracting.
issues.action: Review procurement pipeline; consider pausing non-urgent procurements or using county / national frameworks instead

issues.label: Supplier notification
issues.description: Suppliers with existing contracts will need to be notified of the change of contracting authority.
issues.action: Plan supplier communications as part of LGR transition programme

lgr_considerations.source: No single constitutional source — derived from application of PA2023, ART13, PART3E and CONTRACT-RULES to LGR context.

schema_todos: COMPLIANCE QUESTION (C3): Contracts £100k-£250k — Forward Plan requirement not currently applied in practice. Discuss with MO and s151 Officer. See source_documents.known_conflicts.C3 and risk flag R11.
DOCUMENT UPDATE (C1 and C2): Contract Rules Rules 18.1 and 18.4 conflict with Article 13. Update Contract Rules at next constitutional review.
LGR RISK: Director of One Legal (Tewkesbury s.101) — Tewkesbury itself subject to LGR. This arrangement requires urgent legal and constitutional review before vesting day.
Populate known_framework_providers with Gloucestershire-specific regional frameworks
Add GCC-hosted framework instances when established — currently none
Add framework_id foreign key to contract_record to replace flat framework_reference object
Build framework instance document as peer to contracts register
Assess LGR risk level for all active customer frameworks with call-offs in flight at vesting day
Add council-specific CPV code taxonomy if preferred categories exist
Consider integration with finance system purchase order data
Consider integration with Find a Tender API for automated notice tracking
Consider social value scoring framework as sub-schema
Expand LGR considerations as programme develops
Review Director of One Legal (Tewkesbury s.101) arrangement under LGR — continuation uncertain
Add Appendix 1 (PCR2015) rules once populated — currently blank in source document

source_documents.description: Constitutional and legislative documents from which this schema is derived. Every rule, threshold, role and obligation in this schema is traceable to one or more of these sources. Where documents conflict the higher authority source takes precedence as noted.

documents.title: Procurement Act 2023
documents.notes: Primary legislation governing public procurement. Superseded PCR 2015 for procurements commenced on or after 24 February 2025.

documents.title: Procurement Act 2023 (Threshold Amounts) (Amendment) Regulations 2025
documents.notes: Updated financial thresholds. First revision since Act came into force.

documents.title: Local Government Act 1972, s.135
documents.notes: Requires councils to make standing orders for contracts.

documents.title: National Procurement Policy Statement 2024
documents.notes: s.13(9) PA2023 requires contracting authorities to have regard to the NPPS.

documents.title: Public Services (Social Value) Act 2012
documents.notes: Requires consideration of economic, social and environmental wellbeing in service procurement above threshold.

documents.title: Article 12 — Decision Making
documents.notes: Sets out key decision thresholds and decision-making principles. Source for KD1-KD4 triggers and key decision definition.

documents.title: Article 13 — Finance, Contracts and Legal Matters
documents.notes: Sets out contract execution authority by value. Takes precedence over Contract Rule 18 where they conflict. Source for execution tiers (£25k/£50k thresholds) and Common Seal provisions.
conflicts: Contract Rule 18 states lower signing tier at £30,000 — Article 13.02 states £25,000. Article 13 takes precedence.
Contract Rule 18.4 lists Monitoring Officer as electronic signature approver — Article 13.06 does not. Article 13 takes precedence.

documents.title: Part 3E — Executive Functions (Table 4 and Appendix A)
documents.notes: Leader's scheme of delegation of executive functions. Table 4 is the definitive source for tender acceptance authority by tier (officer/Cabinet Member/Cabinet). Appendix A sets Cabinet Member decision limits. 3E.11 establishes the rule that officers cannot take key decisions without Leader authorisation.
key_rules: 3E.11 — Officers cannot take key decisions without specific Leader authorisation
Table 4 — Accept tenders not exceeding £250,000: MD/D (Officer)
Table 4 — Accept tenders exceeding £250,000: Cabinet Member
Table 4 — Waiver exceeding £250,000: Cabinet Member (or MD urgent)
Appendix A para 2(3) — Cabinet Members cannot make decisions exceeding £250,000 expenditure

documents.title: Proper Officers
documents.notes: Statutory proper officer designations. Source for Head of Paid Service, s151 Officer, and Corporate Director procurement-relevant functions.

documents.title: Contract Rules (PA2023 regime)
documents.notes: Detailed procurement procedure rules. Applies to all contracts entered into on or after 24 February 2025. Contracts and procurements commenced before this date governed by PCR2015 rules at Appendix 1 (not yet populated in source document).

key_sections.Rule 3: Calculation of contract values — inclusive of VAT, whole life including extensions
key_sections.Rule 5: Contract value tiers and procurement routes
key_sections.Rules 18-21: Contract formalities — execution, records, bonds, embedded leases
key_sections.Rules 22-25: Specific methods — frameworks, collaborative procurement, consultants

documents.title: Scheme of Sub-Delegation
documents.notes: Managing Director's sub-delegations to Heads of Service. Source for £250,000 Head of Service award limit, Head of Finance and Resources waiver approval authority, and service area responsibilities.

key_provisions.all_heads: Accept tenders and quotations within budget and not exceeding £250,000
key_provisions.head_of_finance_only: Approve waiver to Contract Rules where value does not exceed £250,000

precedence_order: 5. Constitutional delegation schemes (Part 3E, Scheme of Sub-Delegation)

known_conflicts.issue: Contract execution lower tier threshold: Article 13.02 says £25,000, Contract Rule 18.1 says £30,000
known_conflicts.action_required: Contract Rules to be updated at next review

known_conflicts.issue: Electronic signature approvers: Article 13.06 requires s151 consent having obtained Council Solicitor advice. Contract Rule 18.4 also lists Monitoring Officer.
known_conflicts.resolution: Article 13 takes precedence. Schema reflects Article 13.06.
known_conflicts.action_required: Contract Rules to be updated at next review

known_conflicts.issue: Contracts £100k-£250k are key decisions under Article 12 KD3 requiring Leader authorisation (3E.11), but the Scheme of Sub-Delegation treats £250k as a simple officer award ceiling without reference to key decision status. In practice GCC may not be applying Forward Plan requirements to contracts in this range.
known_conflicts.resolution: All three documents apply simultaneously. Forward Plan entry is the mechanism by which Leader authorises officer award of key decisions (3E.11). Schema reflects this. Practice may not currently match constitutional requirement.
known_conflicts.action_required: Live compliance question — discuss with Monitoring Officer and s151 Officer. Consider whether constitution needs updating to reflect actual practice, or whether practice needs to change.

known_conflicts.issue: Table 4 delegates tender acceptance above £250,000 to Cabinet Member. Appendix A para 2(3) states Cabinet Members cannot make decisions involving expenditure exceeding £250,000. These appear contradictory.
known_conflicts.resolution: Resolved by lex specialis: Table 4 specific delegation overrides Appendix A general ceiling. Cabinet Members may accept tenders above £250,000 (up to £500,000 KD4 threshold) under the specific Table 4 Commissioning and Contracts delegation. Schema reflects this resolved position.
known_conflicts.action_required: None — resolved constitutionally. Consider requesting MO opinion to formally confirm interpretation if this is ever challenged.

procurement_case.description: Models the full procurement lifecycle from need definition through to contract award. Every contract_record should have a parent procurement_case. Source: CONTRACT-RULES Rules 3-17, ART12, PART3E.
procurement_case.source: CONTRACT-RULES Rules 3-17, ART12, PART3E Table 4, SUB-DELEGATION

title.description: Short descriptive title of the procurement

service_area.description: Council service area initiating the procurement

service_id.description: Cross-reference to services schema service_id. Enables service to procurement to contract linkage.

authorised_officer.description: Post title of Authorised Officer responsible for the procurement. Source: CONTRACT-RULES Rule 4.1.

budget_holder.description: Post title of Budget Holder. Must confirm sufficient budget for full contract life before procurement commences. Source: CONTRACT-RULES Rule 4.2.9.

value_estimated_gbp.description: Estimated whole-life contract value inclusive of VAT including all options and extensions. Source: CONTRACT-RULES Rule 3.4.

contract_type.description: Contract type determines applicable threshold. Source: PA2023, SI 2025/1200.

procurement_tier.description: Computed from decision authority matrix using value_estimated_gbp. Determines route, authority and Forward Plan requirement.

procurement_route.description: Procurement route. Must be appropriate for tier and value. Source: CONTRACT-RULES Rule 5.

decision_type.description: Derived from tier. Determines Forward Plan and approval requirements. Source: ART12, PART3E.

key_decision_triggers.description: Applicable key decision triggers. KD1 and KD2 require qualitative assessment by Authorised Officer. KD3 and KD4 are monetary. Source: ART12 12.03(b).

forward_plan_reference.description: Forward Plan entry reference. Required before procurement commences where key_decision is true. Absence is a constitutional compliance failure under 3E.11. Source: PART3E Appendix A, ART12, 3E.11.
